name: Polenta Crostini with Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh brown mushrooms (such as cremini or portobello), well cleaned and sliced
recipeInstructions:
The use of instant polenta in this recipe from Carol Fields' "Italy in Small Bites," is strictly optional. If you prefer, make it the traditional way. Just be sure to warm up your stirring arm in advance.
Soak dried mushrooms in warm water to cover for at least 45 minutes or until softened. Remove from liquid carefully and rinse well under cold running water to remove any sand still clinging. Chop mushrooms roughly and drain thoroughly. Strain soaking liquid twice through a sieve lined with cheesecloth or paper towels, and reserve for use in another dish.
Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet and sauté onion until translucent and tender, 10-15 minutes. Add garlic and all mushrooms, reduce heat to low and cook, stirring intermittently, for up to 20 minutes, until tender. Add tomatoes, parsley, salt, and pepper to taste. Continue cooking another 5 minutes.
Cut Quick Polenta into slices 2-inch wide and 3-4 inches long and brush lightly with olive oil. Broil until firm and lightly crisp on both sides. Place a spoonful of hot mushroom sauce on top of each crostino and serve.
